mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 24-month rolling window or positive return frequency drops below 48% during rate cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 55% of triggered instances or the threshold fails to separate return distributions at the 5% significance level.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital demand growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce a positive return frequency above 58% or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation crosses above 2.5 percent annually, the Federal Reserve restricts liquidity, causing immediate negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week due to anticipated monetary tightening.
failure_condition: The signal produces positive returns in > 60% of high-inflation observations or loses predictive power when CPIAUCSL remains below 2.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect expanding manufacturing capacity and corporate output, which directly translates to higher earnings expectations and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.01 over any 24-month rolling window or positive return frequency drops below 48% during expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in total nonfarm payrolls exceeding 100,000 signals abrupt labor market contraction and reduced aggregate demand, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 55% of triggered instances or the threshold fails to separate return distributions at the 5% significance level.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in real GDP and retail sales confirms broad-based economic expansion, reinforcing corporate revenue projections and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields a negative return frequency above 50% or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for monetary policy variables.

mechanism_narrative: Stagnant industrial production combined with persistent inflation creates a stagflationary environment that compresses profit margins and triggers immediate negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails to predict negative returns when inflation is below 1.5 percent or produces positive returns in > 65% of triggered observations.
